evidence: The docstring asks for the largest A in R^n such that 'every three points from A determine an isosceles triangle', which is exactly the definition of an isosceles set.
The docstring's defining condition is that any three points of A 'determine an isosceles triangle' (two of the three pairwise distances are equal).
The module header names the problem '**Borsuk's conjecture** (1933)' and the decl formally states its resolution (false for some n, per Kahn-Kalai [KK93]).
Docstring: '**Borsuk's conjecture, small dimensions**' — the n <= 3 case proved by Eggleston [Eg55].

The file's references ('A new upper bound for the Heilbronn triangle problem') and the problems.yaml comment 'Heilbronn's triangle problem' identify erdos_507's alpha(n) estimation as exactly the Heilbronn triangle problem.
Docstring 'Estimate a lower bound for alpha(n)' — the lower-bound half of the Heilbronn triangle problem [KPS82].
Docstring 'Estimate an upper bound for alpha(n)' — the upper-bound half of the Heilbronn triangle problem [CPZ24].

Docstring: 'The Hadwiger–Nelson problem asks: How many colors are required to color the plane such that no two points at distance 1 from each other have the same color?' — the decl states exactly this problem.
The statement is chi(R^2) = ?, defined via SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber of the unit-distance plane graph.
Docstring: 'Aubrey de Grey improved the lower bound for the chromatic number of the plane to 5 in 2018' — the de Grey lower bound for the Hadwiger–Nelson problem.
Docstring: 'The chromatic number of the plane is at least 4', proven via the Moser spindle or Golomb graph — a classical bound in the Hadwiger–Nelson problem.
The set {z in C : |f(z)| <= 1} for monic polynomial f whose disc-cover is asked about is a polynomial lemniscate (sublevel set of |f|).
The docstring asks for an infinite set A such that every a in A has an integer n with phi(n) = a — the statement is about preimages of Euler's totient function.
Docstring: 'Let f be a transcendental entire function' — the supremum of the liminf ratio ranges over entire (Differentiable C) functions.
The statement quantifies over f with 'Transcendental C[X] f' — transcendental (non-polynomial) entire functions.
Docstring: 'Let f = sum a_k z^(n_k) be an entire function of finite order' — a min-modulus/max-modulus gap theorem for entire functions [Fu63].
The hypothesis 'HasFabryGaps n' (n_k / k -> infinity) is the Fabry gap condition, named for the Fabry gap theorem.
